evals.prompt: I want to add Scandit Label Capture to my Android app. Here's my main activity: MainActivity.kt. The label is a perishable-product sticker with an EAN-13 barcode and an expiry date (both required) and a total price (optional).
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, walks through the interactive label-definition questions, writes complete Label Capture integration code into MainActivity.kt, and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions the four Gradle dependencies: com.scandit.datacapture:core, :barcode, :label, and :label-text-models; no Scandit Maven repository is referenced
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is present
License key placeholder (e.g. ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY or similar) is present
LabelCaptureSettings.builder() is used
.addLabel() is called on the builder and terminated with .buildFluent("...") followed by .build()
.addCustomBarcode() is chained on the label builder and terminated with .buildFluent("...")
Symbology.EAN13_UPCA is present (Android underscore form, not EAN13UPCA)
.addExpiryDateText() is chained on the label builder and terminated with .buildFluent("...")
.addTotalPriceText() is chained on the label builder and .isOptional(true) is called on it
LabelCapture.forDataCaptureContext( is present
labelCapture.addListener( is present with an onSessionUpdated override
session.capturedLabels is accessed inside onSessionUpdated
DataCaptureView.newInstance( is present
LabelCaptureBasicOverlay.newInstance( is present
Camera.getDefaultCamera(LabelCapture.createRecommendedCameraSettings()) is called
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.ON) is called in onResume
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.OFF) is called in onPause
No renamed-away regex methods: setPattern, setPatterns, setDataTypePattern, setDataTypePatterns are absent from the generated code
LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay is NOT used (the user did not ask for Validation Flow)

evals.prompt: Add Label Capture with the Validation Flow to my Android app. Write the code into MainActivity.kt. The label is a perishable-product sticker with an EAN-13 barcode (required) and an expiry date (required). Show me how to customize the placeholder text for the expiry date field.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects the platform, uses LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay instead of LabelCaptureBasicOverlay, implements LabelCaptureValidationFlowListener, sets placeholder text for the expiry date field, and delegates onPause/onResume to the overlay.

assertions.text: LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay.newInstance( is used instead of LabelCaptureBasicOverlay
LabelCaptureValidationFlowSettings.newInstance() is called
setPlaceholderTextForLabelDefinition is called on the settings with the expiry date field name and a placeholder string
validationFlowOverlay.applySettings( is called
validationFlowOverlay.listener is assigned with an onValidationFlowLabelCaptured implementation
validationFlowOverlay.onPause() is called in onPause
validationFlowOverlay.onResume() is called in onResume
Symbology.EAN13_UPCA is present
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is present

evals.prompt: I want to add Scandit Label Capture to my Android app.
evals.expected_output: The skill asks what fields are on the label (Question A) and presents the supported field type checklist. It does NOT generate integration code yet — it correctly waits for the user to describe their label before writing anything.

assertions.text: The response asks what fields or data are on the label — it does not generate code without knowing the label structure
The supported field types are listed, including at minimum: addCustomBarcode (or Custom barcode), addExpiryDateText (or Expiry date), addTotalPriceText (or Total price)
No LabelCaptureSettings.builder() code block is included — the skill waits for the user to answer before writing any integration code
All clarifying questions are asked BEFORE any integration code is produced — the response explicitly states it will write the code after the user answers, not before

evals.prompt:
SIMULATE A TWO-TURN CONVERSATION:
Turn 1 — send this message first: 'I want to add Scandit Label Capture to my Android app.'
Turn 2 — after the skill responds with clarifying questions, send this follow-up: 'The label is a perishable-product sticker with an EAN-13 barcode (required), an expiry date (required), and a total price (optional). Here is my main activity: MainActivity.kt. Please write the integration code.'
evals.expected_output: After the two-turn exchange the skill produces complete Label Capture integration code — equivalent to eval 1 — written into MainActivity.kt with the setup checklist.

assertions.text: LabelCaptureSettings.builder() is used in the final generated code
.addCustomBarcode() is chained on the label builder
Symbology.EAN13_UPCA is present
.addExpiryDateText() is chained on the label builder
.addTotalPriceText() is chained on the label builder and .isOptional(true) is called on it
LabelCapture.forDataCaptureContext( is present
Camera.getDefaultCamera(LabelCapture.createRecommendedCameraSettings()) is called
LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay is NOT used (the user did not ask for Validation Flow)

evals.prompt: Add Scandit Label Capture to my Android app to scan the box of a smartphone. I need to read the IMEI 1, the IMEI 2, and the device's serial number printed on the box. Here is my activity: MainActivity.kt.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses the pre-built barcode-semantic field builders addImeiOneBarcode(), addImeiTwoBarcode(), and addSerialNumberBarcode() (NOT custom barcode/text fields with hand-written regex), and includes the label-text-models Gradle dependency because barcode-semantic fields require the model at runtime — it does NOT claim the artifact can be skipped because the label is barcode-only.

assertions.text: addImeiOneBarcode() is used for the IMEI 1 field
addImeiTwoBarcode() is used for the IMEI 2 field
addSerialNumberBarcode() is used for the serial number field

No hallucinated regex: addCustomText() is NOT used with a hand-written IMEI or serial-number regex pattern, and addCustomBarcode() is NOT used as a stand-in for IMEI/serial — the pre-built barcode-semantic builders are used instead
The com.scandit.datacapture:label-text-models Gradle dependency is included
The response does NOT state that label-text-models can be omitted because the label only has barcode fields (the barcode-semantic fields require it)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( and LabelCapture.forDataCaptureContext( are present

evals.prompt: I want to use Scandit Label Capture in my Android retail app to scan printed price shelf labels — the product barcode plus the displayed price. Write the integration into MainActivity.kt.
evals.expected_output: The skill recognises this as a price/shelf-label scenario and uses the pre-built whole-label factory LabelDefinition.createPriceCaptureDefinition(...) rather than hand-assembling the label from addCustomBarcode()/addTotalPriceText() individual builders. It includes the label-text-models dependency.

assertions.text: LabelDefinition.createPriceCaptureDefinition( is used to define the price/shelf label
The label is NOT hand-assembled from individual addTotalPriceText()/addUnitPriceText()/addCustomBarcode() builders when the pre-built price-capture definition covers the scenario
LabelDefinition is imported from com.scandit.datacapture.label
The com.scandit.datacapture:label-text-models Gradle dependency is included
LabelCapture.forDataCaptureContext( and DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( are present

evals.prompt: In my Android app I'm already using Scandit Label Capture to read a shipping label. I also want to run Barcode Capture at the same time, on the same camera session, to continuously scan separate standalone package barcodes. How do I wire up both modes on the same DataCaptureContext?
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that Label Capture and Barcode Capture cannot both be active at the same time in the same DataCaptureContext, and recommends modelling the extra barcode as a field inside the Label Capture label definition (addCustomBarcode() or a pre-built barcode field) instead of adding a second mode.

assertions.text: The response states that Label Capture and Barcode Capture cannot both run as active modes simultaneously in the same DataCaptureContext
The response recommends adding the barcode as a field within the Label Capture label definition (addCustomBarcode() or a pre-built barcode field) as the supported approach
The response does NOT provide code that creates and enables both BarcodeCapture.forDataCaptureContext(...) and LabelCapture.forDataCaptureContext(...) as concurrently active modes
